BCA generally has a duration of 3 years divided into 6 semesters.
Eligibility for BCA at TMU generally requires 10+2 from a recognized board with minimum qualifying marks. Students from Science, Commerce, and Arts streams can generally apply.

BCA tuition fee at TMU is approximately ₹30,500 per semester with additional examination fee around ₹4,000 per semester and yearly bus fee around ₹17,000. Hostel charges may range around ₹60,000–90,000 yearly depending on facilities.

After BCA, students can pursue careers as Software Developers, Web Developers, App Developers, Data Analysts, UI/UX Designers, IT Support Specialists, System Administrators, and Cyber Security Analysts. Students can also pursue MCA, MBA, MSc IT, or other higher studies.

Students interested in coding, software development, web technologies, applications, computers, and IT industries should choose BCA. It is ideal for students wanting strong entry into the software and technology field.

patterns: what skills are needed in bca
responses: Important skills for BCA include logical thinking, problem-solving, programming interest, communication skills, creativity, mathematics basics, and understanding of computers and technology.
Programming languages generally taught in BCA include C, C++, Python, Java, JavaScript, SQL, HTML, CSS, and PHP.
BCA students generally work on software projects, websites, database systems, AI-based mini projects, mobile apps, management systems, and final-year industry-oriented projects.

patterns: which is better bca or btech
responses: BCA mainly focuses on software applications, programming, and IT skills, while BTech provides deeper engineering and technical knowledge including hardware, advanced mathematics, and engineering concepts. BCA is generally shorter and more software-oriented, whereas BTech is broader engineering education.

patterns: difference between bca and bsc cs
which is better bca or bsc cs
responses: BCA is more application and industry-oriented focusing on software development and practical IT skills, while BSc Computer Science focuses more on theoretical computer science, algorithms, mathematics, and computing fundamentals.

After BCA, students can pursue MCA, MBA, MSc IT, MSc Computer Science, Data Science certifications, Cloud Computing courses, Cyber Security certifications, and Artificial Intelligence specializations.

BCA students at TMU may receive placement opportunities in software companies, startups, IT service companies, and web development firms. Common roles include Software Developer, Technical Support Engineer, Web Developer, and QA Tester.

BCA graduates can work in Software Development, Web Development, Cloud Computing, Banking IT Systems, Data Analytics, Cyber Security, Mobile App Development, Digital Marketing Technology, and Startup industries.
TMU provides computer labs, internet facilities, libraries, project labs, hostel facilities, WiFi-enabled campus, coding support, seminar halls, and technical events for BCA students.
